breaking.text: CrowdStrike slips ~4% after hours despite Q4 earnings beat as guidance disappoints some investors
Broadcom surges ~5% after hours on record Q1 revenue of $19.3B, AI revenue up 106% YoY to $8.4B with $100B AI chip forecast
Dow futures slip ~270 points overnight as post-rally momentum fades ahead of jobless claims data
Broadcom surges 5% after hours on record $19.3B Q1 revenue, projects $100B AI chip revenue by 2027
Treasury Sec. Bessent confirms 15% global tariff takes effect this week, replacing prior 10% rate under Section 122

summary: Wall Street rallied on Wednesday March 4 with the S&P 500 gaining 0.78% to 6,869.50 and the Nasdaq jumping 1.29%, driven by a tech rebound as oil prices pulled back from Iran conflict highs and fears of an economic growth scare faded. Coinbase surged 15% after President Trump signaled support for the digital asset market structure bill and sided with crypto firms over banks on stablecoin yield, while Moderna rose 12% on a $2.25B Covid vaccine patent settlement. After the bell, Broadcom delivered a blockbuster Q1 report with record $19.3B revenue and AI revenue of $8.4B growing 106% YoY, guiding Q2 AI revenue to $10.7B and announcing a $10B buyback — while CrowdStrike beat on revenue but slipped 4% after hours on cautious guidance. Markets now eye Treasury Secretary Bessent's confirmation that 15% global tariffs will take effect this week and Friday's February nonfarm payrolls report.

highlights.text: February nonfarm payrolls report due Friday March 6 — after January showed only 130K jobs added and unemployment at 4.3%
Ross Stores pops 7% on Q4 earnings beat — $2.00 EPS vs $1.90 estimate, $6.64B revenue vs $6.42B expected
Moderna jumps 12% after agreeing to pay up to $2.25B ($950M upfront + $1.3B contingent) to settle Arbutus/Genevant Covid vaccine patent lawsuit
Coinbase surges 15% as Trump urges passage of CLARITY Act and sides with crypto firms against banks on stablecoin yield — CEO Armstrong met with Trump at White House
CrowdStrike posts Q4 revenue of $1.31B (+23%) and record $331M net new ARR (+47% YoY), but stock drops ~4% after hours on growth deceleration concerns
Broadcom reports record Q1 revenue of $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI revenue of $8.4B (+106%), and guides Q2 AI revenue to $10.7B (+140% YoY) — announces $10B share buyback
Thursday's economic calendar heavy with labor data: Challenger job cuts, initial jobless claims, revised Q4 productivity and unit labor costs — all ahead of Friday's nonfarm payrolls
Cracker Barrel pops 9% after-hours on surprise Q2 earnings beat: EPS of $0.25 vs consensus loss of -$0.20, revenue $874.8M tops estimates
Moderna rallies nearly 11% after agreeing to pay up to $2.25 billion to settle Covid vaccine patent dispute with Biopharma Corp and Genevant Sciences
Coinbase surges 14% and MicroStrategy jumps 10% as Bitcoin reclaims $73,000 on Trump administration support for stablecoin Clarity Act legislation
Broadcom posts record Q1 revenue of $19.3B (+29% YoY), AI revenue soars 106% to $8.4B; CEO projects $100B+ AI chip revenue in 2027 with OpenAI as new sixth customer
Key economic data due March 5: Initial Jobless Claims (8:30 AM), Q4 revised Unit Labor Costs & Nonfarm Productivity, and Challenger Job Cuts report
StubHub posts $1.9B net loss in first post-IPO earnings on $1.4B stock-based compensation charge, but guides FY2026 to $9.9-10.1B GMS and $400-420M adjusted EBITDA
Treasury Secretary Bessent says 15% global tariffs under Section 122 take effect this week, but predicts rates return to pre-Supreme Court ruling levels within 5 months
Veeva Systems beats Q4 estimates with $836M revenue (+13% YoY) and $2.06 EPS vs $1.93 consensus, maintaining 40%+ operating margins — shares up 4% after hours
Broadcom CEO Hock Tan outlines path to $100B in AI chip revenue, citing progress with Anthropic, Meta, and OpenAI as key custom accelerator customers
